zh.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Jordan於1720年出生。她於1740年與John Kinkead在美國賓夕法尼亞州波藍德卡萊爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1792年在美國賓夕法尼亞州波藍德享年72歲。

nameMeanings.name: Jordan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, German, French (mainly Alsace and Haute-Savoie), Polish, Czech, and Slovenian; Spanish and Hungarian (Jordán): from the Christian personal name or nickname Jordan. This is taken from the name of the river Jordan (Hebrew Yarden, a derivative of yarad ‘to go down’, i.e. to the Dead Sea). At the time of the Crusades it was a common practice for crusaders and pilgrims to bring back flasks of water from the river in which John the Baptist had baptized people, including Christ himself, and to use it in the christening of their own children. As a result Jordan became quite a common personal name.

variants.name: Giordano
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Salvatore, Rocco, Antonio, Pasquale, Carmine, Vito, Domenic, Gaetano, Giuseppe, Sal, Santo.
Italian: from the personal name Giordano, an equivalent of Jordan .

variants.name: Jourdan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Dominique, Gabrielle, Henri, Lucien, Pierre, Raoul, Yves.
English: variant of   Jordan  .
French (mainly southern): variant of   Jourdain  , a cognate of 2 below.

variants.name: Jourdain
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French/Haitian Henri, Pierre, Fritz, Hans, Jacques, Rommel.
French, Breton (mainly Finistère), and West Indian (mainly Haiti, also Martinique and Dominican Republic): from the French personal name Jourdain, an equivalent of Jordan .
English (London, Derbyshire): French-influenced variant of   Jordan  ; compare 1 above.

variants.name: Jordison
meanings: English (North Yorkshire, Durham, and Northumberland): from a Middle English pet form, Jordi, of the male personal name Jordan + -son, or directly from a phonetic shortening of Jordanson.

variants.name: Jorden
meanings: English: variant of   Jordan  .
North German (mainly Jörden): probably a variant of Jordan . Compare Yerden and Yerdon .

variants.name: Judd
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Judde, Jutte, a pet form of Jordan .
German: variant of   Jude  .

variants.name: Jorda
meanings: Catalan (Jordà): from the personal name Jordà (see Jordan ).

variants.name: Jerden
meanings: English: from a variant of the personal name Jurdon, an old form of Jordan .

variants.name: Gordan
meanings: German: variant of Jordan . The surname Gordan is very rare in Germany.
Irish (Wexford and Sligo): adopted as an Anglicized form of Mag Mhuirneacháin ‘son of Muirneachán’, a personal name from a diminutive of muirneach ‘lovable’.

variants.name: Jordon
meanings: English: variant of   Jordan  .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.description: Historical Boundaries 1750: Cumberland, Pennsylvania Colony, British Colonial America 1776: Cumberland, Pennsylvania, United States
Thomas Jefferson's American Declaration of Independence endorsed by Congress. Colonies declare independence.
The Declaration of Independence was signed in Philadelphia on July 4, 1776. The liberty bell was first rung here to Celebrate this important document.

en.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Jordan was born in 1720. She married John Kinkead in 1740, in Carlisle, Cumberland, Pennsylvania, United States. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1792, in Cumberland, Pennsylvania, United States, at the age of 72.

fr.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Jordan est née en 1720. Elle a épousé John Kinkead en 1740, à Carlisle, Cumberland, Pennsylvanie, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1792, à Cumberland, Pennsylvanie, États-Unis, à l’âge de 72 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Jordan wurde 1720 geboren. Sie hat 1740 in Carlisle, Cumberland, Pennsylvania, Vereinigte Staaten John Kinkead geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1792 im Alter von 72 Jahren in Cumberland, Pennsylvania, Vereinigte Staaten.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Jordanは1720年に生まれた。1740年にアメリカ合衆国ペンシルベニア州クウンバーランドカーリスルでJohn Kinkeadと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1792年にアメリカ合衆国ペンシルベニア州クウンバーランドで72歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Jordan(은)는 1720년 태어났다. 그녀는 1740년 미국 펜실베이니아 주 c벌랜드 칼리슬일에서 John Kinkead(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1792년 미국 펜실베이니아 주 c벌랜드에서 72세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Jordan родилась в 1720 г. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Kinkead в 1740 г. в местности карлисл, кумбирланд, Пенсильвания, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1792 г. в местности кумбирланд, Пенсильвания, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 72 лет.
